package_name: Budget Beacon Financial Seed Data - VA Assistance Version
intended_use: Populate the user's local Budget Beacon application and its Pay Path/Stash Map modules with currently available household budget data for VA/utility bill assistance planning.
important_agent_instruction: Do not invent missing account numbers, balances, due dates, or creditor data. Use zero for non-applicable categories and mark unconfirmed items as estimated or not_provided. Preserve the distinction between applicant-only income and estimated household income.

profiles.relationship_to_household: Applicant, disabled veteran, Eversource account holder, primarily responsible for electric utility payment.
profiles.notes: Receives SSDI/Social Security and VA disability compensation. Food and finances are separated from partner where practical.

profiles.relationship_to_household: Significant other / same property. Not legally married to applicant. Maintains separate food and finances, has separate living space, contributes to shared bills as able, and performs property-management role in lieu of rent.
profiles.notes: Estimated part-time Market Basket income pending pay stubs. Food benefits are estimated and should be confirmed by benefit paperwork.

self.source_note: Recurring SSA TREAS 310 deposits shown on January, February, and March 2026 Chime checking statements. Official SSA award letter should be attached later if available.
VA Benefit Summary Letter dated 2026-04-18 lists 30% combined service-connected evaluation and current monthly award amount of $552.47.

partner.category: Net Wages - Market Basket dairy stocker, part-time
partner.source_note: $300/week x 52 / 12 = $1,300/month. Replace with pay-stub average when available.
User stated approximately $75-$100/month. Use $75 as conservative estimate unless later proof shows otherwise.

totals.note: Use applicant-only and household scenarios separately if Budget Beacon supports multiple views. Assistance program may decide whether partner income counts.

housing_and_property.notes: No rent payment listed for applicant. Partner pays no rent; property-management services are in lieu of rent.

utilities.notes: Primary assistance target. User stated payment plan is $665/month, but affordable amount is approximately $400/month, leaving a $265/month gap. Latest uploaded Eversource bill shows $5,248.58 total due and $585.74 current charges.
Trash company: NOT recycling. Current amount owed approximately $165 due to arrears.
Artesian well; no separate water bill.
No gas/oil heat bill provided; heating is pellets plus electric.
Summer approximately $50-$100/month; winter approximately $300+/month. Use $175/month average; consider seasonal budget mode.

transportation.notes: USAA insurance payment of $30.15 appears in February 2026 Chime statement.
User stated approximately $50/week; partner often handles this from her funds.

food_household_pet.notes: Baseline $200/week. Actual range can reach $300/week depending on available cash. Food/finances are mostly separated; partner SNAP should be tracked separately.
User stated toothpaste, cleaning supplies, and similar items are approximately $50/week; often handled by partner.

debts_loans_and_rentals.notes: March statement showed Rent-A-Center purchase/payment of $220.95; user stated approximately $230/month.
User stated approximately $400/month. Replace with exact statement amount when available.
Total owed to family approximately $400. User can pay $100-$200 increments; use $100/month default until stabilized.
User believes partner student loans are around $200/month. Confirm from partner bank/student-loan statement.
User and partner have debts/collections but no reliable required minimum payment currently documented.

Applicant debts around $2,000 and partner debts in excess of $2,000, but no required monthly payment currently documented. Track as debt inventory, not active monthly obligation, unless payment agreement exists.

subscriptions_security_discretionary.notes: User believes it should be $10 but may currently be around $30; track for verification/cancellation review.

subscriptions_security_discretionary.category: Subscriptions - Amazon Prime and AI/hobby tools
subscriptions_security_discretionary.notes: Combination of Amazon Prime and AI subscriptions used for hobby/flash-work-related activity; not current formal employment.
Small food/fast-food charges appear in statements; set low baseline.
No specific amount provided; low practical estimate.
No practical savings capacity while electric arrears/payment plan remain unresolved.

totals.note: These totals use the active required/estimated obligations listed above. Budget Beacon should allow editing after pay stubs and exact creditor statements are uploaded.

arrears_and_balances.notes: Latest uploaded Eversource bill dated 2026-04-16 shows total due $5,248.58; assistance target is avoiding shutoff and reducing required $665/month payment plan toward affordable $400/month.
Can pay in $100-$200 increments depending on budget.
No current required monthly payment confirmed.
User stated partner likely has more than $2,000 debt; no current required monthly payment confirmed.

pay_path_priorities.action: Flag as urgent assistance target. Track arrears balance and any VA/CAP/EAP/medical-protection assistance. Avoid shutoff.
Track as essential housing/property obligation. Consider veterans tax credit/abatement/deferment workflow separately.
Preserve transportation needed for appointments, errands, and household stability.

pay_path_priorities.item: Food, pet food, and household supplies
pay_path_priorities.action: Track separately from partner SNAP and partner-paid supplies.
Maintain sanitation, communication, and access to benefits/telehealth/VA coordination.
Review for payoff, renegotiation, return, or hardship reduction because this is large relative to income.
Review for cancellation/pause during electric hardship unless needed for income-generating work.

stash_map_goals.notes: Three-month reserve for the gap between affordable $400 and required $665 payment plan.
Build outside peak winter months if possible.
Small catch-up target in addition to regular trash billing if cashflow allows.
Only if electric assistance stabilizes cashflow.

data_quality_and_followups: Attach official SSDI/SSA benefit letter when available.
Attach Kehley M. Smith's Market Basket pay stubs and SNAP/food benefit verification if the program counts household income.
Confirm exact Aaron's monthly payment and balance from statement.
Confirm SimpliSafe actual monthly amount ($10 vs $30).
Confirm exact property tax monthly equivalent and any veteran tax credit/abatement/deferment options.
Update current bank balances before submitting any means-tested asset disclosure. March statements show historical balances only.
